title: Crew member on sunken vessel off Cape Cod has died, officials identify him
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Angel Nieves, 37, of New Bedford was found deceased after the Yankee Rose capsized two miles off Provincetown.
The search for the other missing crew member has ended due to poor weather conditions, officials reported.
The Yankee Rose had a prior emergency on February 26 involving carbon monoxide exposure for eight people.

title: Patriots reportedly among teams interested in Packers’ leading WR in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots are monitoring Packers' wide receiver Romeo Doubs ahead of NFL free agency, according to ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.
Doubs, who led the Packers with 724 receiving yards and six touchdowns in 2025, attracts interest from 49ers, Commanders, and Titans.
ESPN ranks Doubs as the third-highest wide receiver free agent, projecting a potential three-year, $49 million contract.

title: NH cold case solved 40 years after police found man’s skull in woods
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Warren Kuchinsky was identified as the man whose skull was found in 1986 in Bristol, New Hampshire.
The DNA Doe Project helped solve the case using forensic genetic genealogy, confirming Kuchinsky's identity with DNA from a relative.
Authorities found no evidence of foul play in Kuchinsky’s death, whose last known life records were from the mid-1970s.

title: Boston police issue scam warning for fake parking violations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police warn of a scam involving fake parking violation notices appearing to be from Boston Municipal Court.
The fraudulent notices include a judge's name, court details, and a QR code for payment, used in the scam.
Recipients should verify notices through the city's official website and report fraudulent ones to the police immediately.

title: Mansfield man charged with illegally possessing ammunition, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Michael Kennedy, 48, from Mansfield, arrested for possessing ammunition unlawfully as a convicted felon, remains in federal custody.
Investigators found firearm parts, ammunition, and a loaded pistol in Kennedy's home during a search while under supervised release.
Kennedy, previously convicted of multiple felonies, could face up to 15 years in prison if found guilty of the current charges.

title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward has impressed with 10 hits and 12 stolen bases in 12 spring training games.
Max Ferguson leads Red Sox in RBIs with 7, showcasing positional versatility in spring training.
Tayron Guerrero shows potential in bullpen with 6 strikeouts in 6 innings, allowing one earned run.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson ranks second on the Bruins in 5-on-5 goals with 13 and third in 5-on-5 points at 25.
Bruins opted not to trade Arvidsson despite his pending free agent status, valuing his contribution and team cohesion.
Bruins may consider a three-year extension for Arvidsson, needing stability and production in their middle-six forwards.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears, creating a need for a new left guard this offseason.
Free agent Isaac Seumalo, former Steelers guard, is regarded as the top candidate to replace Bradbury's position.
Potential draft pick Emmanuel Pregnon from Oregon is considered a strong option for Patriots' interior offensive line.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support children served by Make-A-Wish in Greater Massachusetts-Rhode Island.
Perry, 48, from Stockton, California, finds inspiration in the resilience and hope of the Make-A-Wish children.
He has been involved with Make-A-Wish for 15 years, meeting many children whose lives and challenges motivate his fundraising efforts.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots are predicted to sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe for three years, $48 million with $25 million guaranteed.
Mafe recorded 44 pressures and a 17.4% pass rush win rate during Seahawks' Super Bowl run last season.
Chargers likely to sign Colts receiver Alec Pierce with a projected three-year, $80 million contract, despite Patriots' interest.
